compiled_state.title: Reverse-delegate purge-non-project-tickets.sh: move implementation to ticket-purge-bridge.sh, make thin wrapper
compiled_state.author: Worktree
compiled_state.description:
## Task

Apply the reverse-delegation pattern (same as f3ce-beaf) to purge-non-project-tickets.sh:
1. Create `plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-purge-bridge.sh` with the full implementation body from purge-non-project-tickets.sh
2. Update the dispatcher (`plugins/dso/scripts/ticket`) at line 402 to exec to `ticket-purge-bridge.sh` instead of `purge-non-project-tickets.sh`
3. Replace purge-non-project-tickets.sh body with a thin exec wrapper that calls the shim

## Thin Wrapper Content (purge-non-project-tickets.sh)

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# purge-non-project-tickets.sh — thin wrapper; canonical implementation in ticket-purge-bridge.sh
set -euo pipefail
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
exec "$SCRIPT_DIR/ticket-purge-bridge.sh" "$@"
```

## Dispatcher Update

In `plugins/dso/scripts/ticket` at the `purge-bridge)` case:
- Change: `exec bash "$SCRIPT_DIR/purge-non-project-tickets.sh" "$@"`
- To: `exec bash "$SCRIPT_DIR/ticket-purge-bridge.sh" "$@"`

## Allowlist Entry

Add `plugins/dso/scripts/purge-non-project-tickets.sh` to `.claude/hooks/pre-commit/check-tickets-boundary-allowlist.conf` with inline comment `# thin wrapper — epic e1b2-2653`.

## Verification

After implementation:
- `.claude/scripts/dso ticket purge-bridge --keep=TEST --dry-run` executes without error
- The existing test suite for purge-non-project-tickets.sh passes (if one exists)
- grep confirms no `purge-non-project-tickets.sh` reference in the new ticket-purge-bridge.sh that would create circular deps

## testing_mode: GREEN
This is a refactor — the existing test suite (if any) validates behavioral equivalence. No new behavior introduced.
